description: When dependencies are updated via bd dep add/remove, the git sync commits don't include meaningful messages about what changed. Should capture the operation in the commit message for traceability.
title: Dep updates don't get commit messages

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `MIGRATION.md` documents rollback using automatic backup refs as well as optional manual restore handles.
- [ ] `MIGRATION.md` no longer implies `skipped_no_remote` covers first-publish rewrite cases.
- [ ] Rollout and rollback docs match actual migrate push semantics.
description:
**Problem**
The operator guide still drifts from actual migration behavior in two places. First, local-only rollback examples assume a manual `refs/heads/beads/store-backup` branch exists, even though the implementation also creates automatic backup refs under `refs/beads/backup/<oid>` for rewrites and ref realignments. Second, the `skipped_no_remote` wording still implies it covers any repo where `origin` exists but `refs/heads/beads/store` is absent, but the executor only returns `skipped_no_remote` for no-op cases; when a rewrite is needed it will create and push the remote store ref.

**Files**: MIGRATION.md, docs/plans/2026-03-01-legacy-deps-hard-cutover-design.md
design:
**Design**
Tighten the operator guide around restore handles and push outcomes.

1. Document how to restore from the automatic backup refs created by migration when an existing local store ref moved.
2. Keep manual restore-handle guidance for operators who want an explicit named branch, but do not make the rollback recipe depend on it.
3. Narrow `skipped_no_remote` docs to the cases the implementation actually returns, and distinguish first-publish-to-empty-remote from no-op/no-remote cases.
4. Ensure rollout success criteria stay aligned with the real push disposition behavior.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content: Audit on 2026-03-11 against /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs-legacy-deps-v1-cutover @ 21e0255b: MIGRATION.md now documents restoring from automatic refs/beads/backup/* refs when no manual restore handle exists, and narrows skipped_no_remote to true no-remote or converged-no-remote cases instead of implying all origin-without-remote-ref rewrites skip push. --repo /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs

title: Migration follow-up: fix rollback docs for automatic backup refs and skipped_no_remote semantics

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] MutationEngine::plan no longer accepts origin_seq or returns pre-encoded bytes tied to a seq.
- [ ] WAL append path is the only place that allocates origin_seq for local mutations.
- [ ] Executor no longer holds a WAL index txn open during mutation planning/encoding.
- [ ] Existing mutation and WAL tests pass or are updated to the new flow.
description:
**Problem**
MutationEngine::plan takes origin_seq and returns fully-encoded EventBody bytes. Executor pre-allocates origin_seq via WalIndex and passes it into planning (src/daemon/executor.rs). This leaks WAL sequencing into the planner, forces long-lived SQLite txns during planning/encoding, and violates REALTIME_PLAN §0.5/§5.6 (origin_seq allocated inside WAL append). It is easy to misuse for batching/retries and makes the "event id not assigned yet" invariant impossible to encode in types.

**Files:** src/daemon/mutation_engine.rs, src/daemon/executor.rs, src/daemon/wal/event_wal.rs, src/daemon/wal/index.rs, src/core/event.rs
design:
**Design**
- Introduce an unsequenced event draft type (e.g., EventDraft without origin_seq/bytes) that carries delta + stamps + request_sha256.
- Move origin_seq allocation into the WAL append path (append_local) using WalIndexTxn::next_origin_seq and durable head lookup, then build EventBody with the assigned seq and encode canonical bytes inside append.
- Replace direct next_origin_seq calls in executor with append_local results (EventId + bytes + sha + prev_sha).
- Keep request_sha256 computed from canonicalized mutation request; do not include origin_seq in that digest.
- Update tests to cover seq allocation boundaries and idempotent retries.
title: Decouple mutation planning from origin_seq allocation

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] No WAL-facing APIs use raw `u64` for origin sequence inside the codebase.
- [ ] Record header encoding/decoding remains identical on the wire.
- [ ] Executor/WAL range paths no longer need to validate `Seq1::from_u64`.
description:
**Problem**
Realtime WAL code uses raw `u64` for origin sequences in multiple layers (`RecordHeader.origin_seq`, wal index APIs, wal range reader). Callers must manually convert to `Seq1` and check for zero. This weakens invariants and makes it easy to forget a check.

**Files**
- src/daemon/wal/record.rs
- src/daemon/wal/index.rs
- src/daemon/wal/event_wal.rs
- src/daemon/repl/runtime.rs
- src/daemon/executor.rs
design:
Refactor WAL APIs to use `Seq1`/`Seq0`:
- `RecordHeader.origin_seq: Seq1` (convert to/from u64 only when encoding/decoding bytes).
- WalIndex `next_origin_seq` returns `Seq1`; `iter_from`/`max_origin_seq` accept/return `Seq0` where appropriate.
- `WalRangeReader::read_range` and any `from_seq_excl` parameters should be `Seq0`.
Update callers and tests accordingly to remove `Seq1::from_u64` checks.
title: WAL: use Seq0/Seq1 types instead of raw u64

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Unit test: apply two label ops out of order; label stamp remains max.
- [ ] Unit test: adding a new dot for an existing label updates label stamp even if membership unchanged.
- [ ] Determinism test: apply same label/dep ops in different orders with different stamps => identical serialized state.
- [ ] Dep stamp behavior mirrors label stamp behavior in tests.
description:
**Problem**
LabelState.stamp (and DepStore.stamp) is assigned directly on membership change; out-of-order apply can regress stamps and updated_stamp. Also, OR-Set internal changes (new dot, cc merge) can occur without membership change but currently do not update stamps.

**Files**: src/core/state.rs, src/core/orset.rs, src/core/bead.rs, src/git/wire.rs
design:
**Design**
- Use max(existing, incoming) for stamp updates to enforce monotonicity.
- Extend OrSetChange to indicate internal state mutation (not only membership diff).
- Update apply_label_add/remove and apply_dep_add/remove to update stamps when internal OR-Set state changes, not just membership diff.

Keep updated_stamp behavior aligned with CRDT_OVERHAUL.md (labels/notes/fields max).
title: Label/dep stamp monotonicity + stamp on internal OR-Set change

acceptance_criteria: - [ ] Unit test: DepKind ordering matches chosen canonical order.\n- [ ] Git wire test: serialized deps.jsonl order is stable and matches canonical order.\n- [ ] CRDT_OVERHAUL.md updated if spec language changes.
description:
**Problem**
DepKind derives Ord by enum order. If spec expects lexical ordering, deps.jsonl sort order may be ambiguous or inconsistent across implementations.

**Files**: src/core/domain.rs, src/git/wire.rs, CRDT_OVERHAUL.md
design:
**Design**
Choose one canonical ordering and enforce it:
- Preferred: implement Ord for DepKind by as_str() lexical order.
- Alternatively: document enum order as canonical and add tests to lock it.
Update docs accordingly.
title: DepKind canonical ordering for deterministic deps.jsonl
